commitMapping: Each entry maps to the Git commit whose subject exactly matches commitSubject.

entries.summary: Established a conflict-safe standalone release repository with one JSON roadmap, machine-readable handoff and changelog, frozen source-input hashes, non-overlapping scope contracts, explicit Git ownership, and release security boundaries.
verification: All 11 JSON documents parsed.
Roadmap validation and next-step query passed.
Dispatch-wave validation passed with no conflicts.
Secret, binary, cache, toolchain, agent-log, and local-input ignore probes passed.
Git diff whitespace validation passed.

entries.summary: Made the pure Common Lisp kernel verifier standalone with fresh semantic and measured SBCL profiles, exact-integer lawful equality saturation, complete positive and fail-closed integration tests, contracts, and a validated Codex skill wrapper.
verification: Verifier integration suite passed 8 of 8 tests under isolated SBCL 2.6.6.
Lawful e-graph suite passed all 13 checks.
Mutation and benchmark mismatch were rejected before timing.
Compiler-warning and output-collision cases failed closed.
Skill metadata passed the skill-creator quick validator.
Core compiler files matched the frozen accepted inputs byte for byte.

entries.summary: Packaged the experimental SBCL-only RQ8 codec and matrix-vector kernel with retained semantic oracles, guarded domain checks, Apache-2.0 ASDF metadata, path-independent direct and test-op gates, and explicit no-performance-claim boundaries.
verification: Direct safety-3 gate passed 62 deterministic checks under isolated SBCL 2.6.6.
ASDF test-op passed the same 62 checks after fresh compilation outside the checkout.
No warning or serious-condition diagnostics escaped either gate.
Exact-scale, unsupported-domain, storage, fallback, bit-equivalence, and nonmutation assertions passed.
Package and core sources remained byte-identical to the frozen inputs.

entries.summary: Packaged the bounded 24-node mirror compiler with immutable semantic graph records, deterministic exact-cover lowering, strict fail-closed generation, a documented public fallback, path-independent ASDF tests, and fresh verifier correctness profiles.
verification: Direct and ASDF gates each passed 37 checks under isolated SBCL 2.6.6.
The ASDF gate passed when invoked from an unrelated working directory.
Twelve verifier cases passed in both fresh semantic and measured correctness profiles without compiler warnings.
Exact cover, incomplete-target rejection, strict runtime rejection, public fallback, stable emission, and bit-level oracle equality passed.
No timing, allocation, portability, whole-system, or material-speedup claim was retained.

entries.summary: Hardened the local alpha with Apache-2.0 licensing and provenance, public usage and security documentation, CI, portable aggregate gates, a deterministic release-content audit, bounded test evidence, exact RQ8 storage arithmetic, and explicit unknown whole-system performance status.
verification: The complete test-all gate passed under SBCL 2.6.6.
The release audit passed over 63 candidate files and 16 JSON documents with zero findings.
All current release files are UTF-8 text without personal absolute paths, secrets, private keys, binaries, generated build artifacts, or runtime safety-0 forms.
README claims match the machine-readable storage, test, portfolio, and reasoned-claim reports.
The robot oracle checkout remained clean.

entries.summary: Published the verified alpha as a public GitHub repository with SSH-authenticated history, an annotated version tag, bounded prerelease notes, exact remote-ref verification, and no expansion of the accepted efficiency claims.
verification: GitHub CLI and SSH authentication succeeded for bibi-chat after the GitHub host key was verified against authenticated metadata.
The public repository origin is git@github.com:bibi-chat/pure-lisp-kernel-lab.git.
The complete SBCL 2.6.6 gate and release audit passed immediately before publication.
Remote main and the peeled annotated v0.1.0-alpha tag resolve to the final step-050 commit.
The GitHub prerelease targets v0.1.0-alpha and preserves every negative claim boundary from docs/RELEASE_NOTES.md.

entries.summary: Replaced the unsupported Ubuntu SBCL 2.2.9 test runtime with a cached build from the hash-pinned official SBCL 2.6.6 source while leaving every semantic, audit, kernel, and claim boundary unchanged.
verification: Workflow parsing and structural assertions confirmed explicit version, source URL, accepted SHA-256, runtime cache, and version check.
The workflow source SHA-256 exactly matched the previously accepted SBCL 2.6.6 toolchain provenance.
The complete local gate passed all six suites on SBCL 2.6.6 and the updated release audit passed with zero findings.
GitHub Actions run 29668603547 passed on the exact implementation commit and identified runtime SBCL 2.6.6.

entries.summary: Closed the hosted-runtime repair from a successful exact-commit GitHub Actions run and restored the main goal to done without changing the published experimental claim boundary.
verification: GitHub Actions run 29668603547 concluded success for commit 5191e112e272010ae88d102dd3bc0f847f57e141.
The hosted log reported SBCL 2.6.6, all six unchanged gates passed, performance_claim=false, and a 64-file/17-JSON zero-error audit.
Roadmap validation returned no remaining next step and dispatch validation returned no active or ready scope.
